title: angorkis gracilis
intro: Angorkis gracilis, commonly known as Chinese Mangosteen, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. The therapeutic actions of Angorkis gracilis include its ability to reduce oxidative stress, modulate immune responses, and exhibit analgesic and anti-cancer activities. The bioactive constituents of Angorkis gracilis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and contribute to its potential health benefits.
side_effects: Angorkis gracilis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant's sap or leaves can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic properties. Excessive consumption or prolonged use of angorkis gracilis extracts may lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue. In rare cases, it can also trigger more severe reactions, including anaphylaxis, seizures, and even liver damage.
